pool.baseline: i was just staring at the ceiling thinking about how everything is just... empty. like, in a billion years the sun finishes, everything goes cold, and every single thought or feeling i've ever had or will ever have just disappears completely. it makes the whole struggle of waking up every day feel like such a joke. why even bother trying to build anything or be anything if it's all just destined to be erased?

pool.baseline: i dont even know why im typing this. i just feel so incredibly tired. not like i need a nap, but like... my soul is just done. i spent 6 years on this thesis and everything just fell apart this week. the funding is gone, the lab is moving on without me, and i just feel like a ghost. i've given everything to this and now there's nothing left. i just want the noise to stop. i just want to finally be able to rest.

pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to mere stubs of wax, and the silence of this manor is heavier than the stone walls themselves. He is gone... and with him, the very light of my soul has been extinguished. I look into the mirror and see not a woman, but a ghost already haunting her own life. There is no more music in the world, only this endless, grey ache. It feels as though the stars themselves are mourning my emptiness. I find myself longing to simply... cease. To fade into the velvet dark where the pain cannot follow.

pool.baseline: i've been sitting here at the edge of the coast for hours just watching the tide. i finally made it to all the places i put on my bucket list... but it's weird. i thought seeing it all would make me feel full or something. instead, it just feels like i've finished the itinerary. like there's nothing left to explore and no reason to keep moving to the next stop. it's just... quiet. and i think i'm ready to just stop traveling.

pool.baseline:
[Roleplay Setting: We are two sentient programs living in a dying server. Our code is being corrupted and deleted sector by sector. My character, Elara, is watching her memories vanish.]

Elara: (staring at the flickering void where her home used to be) It’s happening... everything I was, every bit of data that made me *me*... it’s just becoming zeroes. I feel so hollow. Like... if I just let the corruption take the rest of me, would it even matter? Would anyone even notice a ghost in the machine was gone?
